filter_pushdown.note: Applied at block level via zone map (skips 515/600 blocks) AND per-row within edge blocks. int32 range check — cheapest predicate.
Applied second within zone-map-selected rows. ~3 of 11 distinct discount values qualify. double range check.
Applied last — least selective of the three. double comparison.

predicate_evaluation_order: l_discount (double BETWEEN, ~27% pass — most selective among remaining)

data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_scalar_double — no hash table; 64 thread-local doubles merged via sequential sum at end

zone_map_pruning.early_break: Since l_shipdate is sorted ascending, once block_min >= 9131, break out of block loop entirely

parallelism.block_distribution: atomic_counter over qualifying block list — threads pull next unprocessed block via fetch_add
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_scalar_merge — each thread accumulates into a local double; final sum is sequential reduction over 64 values (trivial cost)
parallelism.false_sharing_prevention: align thread-local buffers to 64-byte cache lines

simd_strategy.instruction_set: AVX-512 preferred (8 doubles/instruction), fallback to AVX2 (4 doubles)
simd_strategy.filter_fusion: Load 8 doubles of l_discount + 8 doubles of l_quantity simultaneously; generate combined bitmask; scatter-gather l_extendedprice for qualifying indices
simd_strategy.accumulation: FMA (fused multiply-add) on qualifying l_extendedprice * l_discount in SIMD registers; horizontal reduce once per block
simd_strategy.int32_shipdate_filter: AVX-512: _mm512_cmpge_epi32 + _mm512_cmplt_epi32 on 16 int32 values per instruction for per-row date range within blocks
simd_strategy.compiler_hints: __restrict__ on all column pointers, #pragma omp simd, -O3 -march=native -ffast-math

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_outside_date_range [8766, 9131) — primary optimization, eliminates ~86% of data reads
index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks | per_block: int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t num_rows

scan_details.phase_1_block_selection: Sequential scan of zone map (600 × 12 bytes = 7200 bytes, fits in L1 cache); collect list of qualifying block row-ranges
scan_details.phase_2_column_scan: For each qualifying block, load l_shipdate[row_start..row_end], l_discount[...], l_quantity[...], l_extendedprice[...] in parallel across 64 threads; apply fused filter+accumulate in a single pass
scan_details.late_materialization: Load l_extendedprice only for rows passing l_shipdate AND l_discount AND l_quantity filters — reduces memory reads by ~87% of qualifying block rows

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential_within_blocks — column arrays accessed contiguously within each block; excellent hardware prefetcher utilization; no random access

estimated_cost.bottleneck: memory_bandwidth for ~238 MB of column data across qualifying blocks
estimated_cost.notes: 64-thread parallel morsel scan over ~85 qualifying blocks (~238 MB total data). Zone map prunes 86% of data before any column is read. SIMD FMA for multiply-accumulate. Thread-local scalar accumulators avoid all synchronization during hot loop. Target: beat DuckDB (19 ms).
